Seed data (src/seed/index.ts:335,418,450) already embeds 'EazyBase' in those metaTitles, producing doubled suffixes like 'Affordable Modular Home Extensions | EazyBase Prefab Extensions UK | EazyBase'; the ?? fallback strings also contain '| EazyBase', so the bug fires even without seeded pages. The other seven page templates (faq, gallery, social, get-a-quote, instant-quote, areas/[slug]) wrap metaTitle in { absolute }, and areas/[slug]/page.tsx:32 has a comment explicitly stating absolute is used to avoid the template doubling the suffix — proving these three are missed omissions, not intent. Not a functional break, but it corrupts the homepage SERP/tab title on an SEO lead-gen site, so 'major' is appropriate.

title: '/#use-cases' links on What We Do point at an anchor that doesn't exist on the home page
detail: USE_CASES cross-links (Kids Playrooms / Home Offices / Dining Rooms / Kitchens) all href '/#use-cases', but the home page's use-case tabs section (page.tsx ~line 143) is a bare <section className="bg-ink-50"> with no id. Scenario: a user on /what-we-do clicks 'Kitchens' expecting to land on the kitchens content and instead lands at the top of the home page with no scroll — the deep link silently degrades on all four links.
suggestedFix: Add id="use-cases" (plus scroll-mt) to the home use-case section, and ideally support selecting the tab via hash/searchParam.

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed by tracing the actual code. src/app/(frontend)/what-we-do/page.tsx lines 78-81 hard-code href '/#use-cases' on four use-case links. On the home page (src/app/(frontend)/page.tsx line 143) the use-case tabs section is a bare <section className="bg-ink-50"> with no id, and a repo-wide grep shows no element anywhere renders id="use-cases" (UseCaseTabs.tsx only emits useId-based tab/panel ids). The codebase's own convention elsewhere (id="process" + scroll-mt-24 on /what-we-do line 215) confirms the anchor mechanism was simply omitted here. Failure path: clicking any of the four links navigates to '/' and lands at the top with no scroll — the deep link silently degrades. Severity 'minor' is appropriate: navigation still works, no error, just four degraded secondary cross-links.

title: Footer renders an unfinished 'Website by —' placeholder credit linking to '#'
detail: The footer bottom bar ships '© {year} Eazybase Extensions Ltd' followed by 'Website by —' with a '{/* TODO: credit + link */}' comment. Scenario: every visitor on every page sees a dangling 'Website by —' line, and clicking the dash jumps to the top of the page — visible placeholder/TODO content in production, which the audit brief explicitly flags.
suggestedFix: Fill in the real credit and URL, or remove the 'Website by' paragraph entirely until the credit is decided.

verdict.reasoning: Verified at src/components/layout/Footer.tsx lines 157-163: the bottom bar unconditionally renders 'Website by' followed by <a href="#"> containing only an em-dash, with an inline '{/* TODO: credit + link */}' comment. The Footer is mounted in src/app/(frontend)/layout.tsx line 43, so this placeholder appears on every frontend page. Clicking the dash navigates to '#' (top of page). No condition hides the paragraph and no data/config supplies a real credit, so the failure path is concrete and unavoidable in production. Severity 'minor' is appropriate — visible unfinished content, but purely cosmetic.

title: Home StatsBar strips the 'roofing' qualifier from the unverified 40-year guarantee claim
detail: The stat renders '40-year guarantee' with the bare label 'guarantee'. Per the content audit (§7 and defect-register item 14) the 40-year figure is roofing-only ('up to a 40 year guarantee' on roofing) and must be verified before reuse; every other surface qualifies it correctly ('Guarantee on roofing, up to' in the seeded statsCounters block, 'roofing guarantee' on /areas, 'Up to a 40-year guarantee on roofing' in QuoteAside). Scenario: a homeowner reads the home page as promising a 40-year guarantee on the whole extension — an overclaim on the site's most prominent trust strip that the company can't honour.
suggestedFix: Change the label to 'roofing guarantee' (and ideally prefix 'up to'), matching the other components.

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed by tracing the render path. src/components/hero/StatsBar.tsx:21 renders `{ value: SITE.stats.guaranteeYears, suffix: '-year', label: 'guarantee' }` = "40-year guarantee" with no roofing qualifier and no "up to". src/lib/site.ts:28 itself annotates the value as roofing-only per audit §7, and docs/CONTENT-AUDIT.md line 173 records the original claim as 'up to a 40 year guarantee' on roofing (defect item 14 says verify before reuse). The hardcoded StatsBar is what the home page actually renders (src/app/(frontend)/page.tsx:127) — the correctly-labelled seeded CMS statsCounters block ('Guarantee on roofing, up to', src/seed/index.ts:318) is used only on about-us/what-we-do, so it does not rescue the home page. All other surfaces qualify the claim (areas/page.tsx:108 'roofing guarantee'; QuoteAside.tsx:14 'Up to a 40-year guarantee on roofing'). The DESIGN-BRIEF counter example ('10-year guarantee') is style guidance, not a sourced claim, so it does not license the unqualified label. Concrete failure: home-page visitors read a whole-extension 40-year guarantee the company only offers (up to) on roofing. Severity minor is appropriate: content/compliance overclaim, one-line label fix, no functional breakage.

title: US spelling 'meters' in the seeded planning-permission FAQ (renders on /faq and in FAQPage schema)
detail: The 'Do I need planning permission?' answer contains 'a 3-meter projection or 4 meters for detached homes' — US spelling carried verbatim from the old site into the seed and confirmed present in eazybase.db, so it renders to users on /faq and is emitted in the FAQPage JSON-LD. The rest of the rebuild consistently uses UK English (the estimator says 'metres'). The audit's copy-quality bar calls for UK English throughout.
suggestedFix: Change to '3-metre projection or 4 metres' in the seed and update the existing FAQ doc in the CMS (the seed skips when docs exist, so the live row needs a direct edit or re-sync).

verdict.reasoning: Verified end-to-end: the US spelling 'a 3-meter projection or 4 meters' exists at src/seed/index.ts:134, is present in the live eazybase.db row (seed skips when docs exist, so the live row needs a direct edit), and the /faq page (src/app/(frontend)/faq/page.tsx) renders the answer to users and emits it verbatim in FAQPage JSON-LD. The rest of the rebuild consistently uses UK 'metres' (InstantQuoteWizard aria-valuetext, pricing.ts clampMetres, area copy). One caveat: the finding overstates the audit — CONTENT-AUDIT.md line 72 says FAQ pairs 'migrate as-is' and no explicit UK-English rule exists — but the seed provably fixes spelling defects inside otherwise-verbatim copy where the audit caught them (the 'compete/competing with electric sockets' fixes at seed lines 323/459 cite the defect register), so 'meters' is the same defect class ('brick and motor') that the audit's typo register simply missed. Real user-facing copy defect, not a style opinion; minor severity is appropriate.

title: Stale/zombie eazybase server occupies port 3000 and hangs all requests
detail: Environmental, not a code defect: a pre-existing node process (PID 4480, started 05/07/2026 15:00 from this repo's node_modules/next/dist/server/lib/start-server.js) is listening on :3000 but never responds (curl -sI times out after 15s). npm run start therefore fails with EADDRINUSE, and the permission system denied killing the unowned process. Verification was performed on port 3001 instead, where the same .next build works perfectly - confirming the hang is a dead process, not an app bug.
suggestedFix: User should kill PID 4480 (taskkill /PID 4480 /F) before running npm run start on port 3000.

verdict.reasoning: Verified live: PID 4480 is a node.exe process started 05/07/2026 15:00:40 running C:\Users\info\GITHUB\eazybase\node_modules\next\dist\server\lib\start-server.js, still listening on port 3000 (Get-NetTCPConnection confirms OwningProcess 4480 on :3000). curl to http://localhost:3000/ with an 8s timeout exits code 28 — the process accepts connections but never responds, so it is genuinely wedged. package.json's start script is plain `next start` (default port 3000), so npm run start will fail with EADDRINUSE until the process is killed. Every claim in the finding is reproducible. Caveat: this is environmental machine state, not a defect in the repo's code — no code change is implied — but it is a real, currently-live blocker of the standard start workflow with a correct, actionable fix (taskkill /PID 4480 /F), and 'minor' is the appropriate grade for an environmental note.
